backfill_note: backfilled 2026-04-26 to satisfy registry schema (raw 77 additive) — axes mirror existing track_a_final_form_axes (7) + track_b_limits_axes (6) = axes_total_surfaced 13
rule: raw:design-strategy-strategy-exploration-omega-cycle (hive raw 47) + raw 25 (environmental coupling — runner argv divergence)
ordinal_stamp: ω (terminal patch — no transcendental jump needed; bounded scope LoC ≤ 20/axis)
purpose: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa line 815 `let mut ai = 2` — hardcoded argv skip. Under remote hexa runner, argv[2] = script path itself (not user flag), so the script path is consumed as `unknown arg` and exit(1) on every invocation. Local runner uses different argv layout. Fix the argv parser robustly without breaking either runner.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 04:25 observed-yesterday symptom: `omega_cycle_atlas_ingest: unknown arg \`tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa\`` on every invocation through remote dispatcher. Parents (meta/roadmap ω-cycles) wanted to absorb but couldn't — environmental blocker.
method: main-thread multi-axis ideation, two-track. Track-A = ≥6 final-form solution axes; Track-B = ≥5 epistemic limits. dedup → Tier-1 ≤ 5 with line-precise loc_estimate (5–20 LoC each, this is small).

context_evidence.surrounding_code: let raw_args = args(); eprintln("DEBUG argc=" + to_string(len(raw_args))); let mut cli = []; let mut ai = 2; while ai < len(raw_args) { cli.push(raw_args[ai]); ai = ai + 1 }
context_evidence.argv_layout_remote: argv[0]=hexa, argv[1]=run, argv[2]=tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, argv[3..]=user_flags (when args() returns full system argv)
context_evidence.argv_layout_local: argv[0]=tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, argv[1..]=user_flags (when stage0 hexa pre-strips runner prefix)

parent_omega_cycles_blocked.role: meta — bridge-tool atlas auto-ingest; 13 bridges → atlas.n6 absorption depends on this tool running
roadmap — phase_2_target=omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa; phase 3/4 evolution path blocked while arg parser bug stands

context_evidence.n6_atlas_absorption_witness_dependency: atlas.append.<cycle_slug>.n6 emission per witness JSON requires successful --absorb invocation; remote-runner unreachable today

track_a_final_form_axes.name: runner_detection_heuristic
track_a_final_form_axes.summary: detect remote vs local runner via env probe (HEXA_RUNNER, HEXA_FORCE_LOCAL) + argv[0] basename check (`hexa` → remote, `*.hexa` → local). branch ai initial value (2 vs 1 vs 0) accordingly.

track_a_final_form_axes.name: self_path_strip
track_a_final_form_axes.summary: scan raw_args; drop any element whose basename equals the script's own basename (`omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa`) AND ends in `.hexa`. runner-agnostic. handles 0/1/multiple occurrences.

track_a_final_form_axes.name: input_mode_flag_override
track_a_final_form_axes.summary: `--input-mode=local|remote|raw` user-explicit override. if present, skip auto-detect entirely. escape hatch when heuristic misfires.

track_a_final_form_axes.name: regression_test_corpus
track_a_final_form_axes.summary: 3-path invocation matrix in _selftest(): (P1) `hexa run tool/...hexa --selftest`; (P2) `hexa run -- tool/...hexa --selftest`; (P3) `HEXA_FORCE_LOCAL=1 hexa run tool/...hexa --selftest` and direct `./tool/...hexa --selftest`. all must exit 0 and emit `__OMEGA_CYCLE_ATLAS_INGEST__ PASS`.

track_a_final_form_axes.name: invocation_shim_wrapper
track_a_final_form_axes.summary: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.sh wraps the real hexa invocation; sets HEXA_RUNNER=remote and forwards $@ verbatim. callers can use shim if direct .hexa invocation flaky. ~20 LoC bash, no hexa change.

track_a_final_form_axes.name: dual_runner_ci_matrix
track_a_final_form_axes.summary: GitHub Actions / local-make matrix: {runner: remote, local} × {invocation_path: P1, P2, P3} = 6 cells. each runs `--selftest`. fail any → block merge. catches future regressions on runner-version bumps.

track_a_final_form_axes.name: args_normalize_helper
track_a_final_form_axes.summary: _normalize_args(raw) → cli — single function encapsulating a1+a2+a3 logic. callers (main, _selftest) share same parser. avoids drift between selftest and main.

track_b_limits_axes.name: open_set_runner_conventions
track_b_limits_axes.summary: cannot enumerate all future hexa runner conventions (stage1, stage2, third-party wrappers, IDE plugins). heuristic fixed at present runner set; new runners may invent argv[k] = config-blob etc.

track_b_limits_axes.name: runner_detect_false_positive
track_b_limits_axes.summary: a1 heuristic mis-classifies if user has a script named `hexa` in PATH that is not the canonical runner, or symlinks. argv[0] basename probe is necessary but not sufficient. acceptable risk only because a3 escape hatch exists.

track_b_limits_axes.name: self_path_strip_is_heuristic
track_b_limits_axes.summary: a2 self-path strip is not a theorem — pathological case: user passes `tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa` AS A DATA ARGUMENT (e.g., as input to a hypothetical --witness-path=… flag). then strip incorrectly drops user data. mitigated only by convention (no current flag accepts a .hexa path), not by proof.

track_b_limits_axes.name: hexa_stage0_args_unstable
track_b_limits_axes.summary: hexa.real / stage0 `args()` semantics not contract-locked across versions. observed: stage0 sometimes returns full system argv, sometimes pre-stripped. version pinning + arg-shape regression test (a4/a6) only — no upstream contract to depend on.

track_b_limits_axes.name: raw_25_environmental_coupling
track_b_limits_axes.summary: this is fundamentally a raw-25 family bug (environmental coupling via implicit argv shape). no cross-version contract from hexa lang spec. parser is defensive, not provably correct. each new runner version requires re-running a4 corpus.

track_b_limits_axes.name: shim_drift_risk
track_b_limits_axes.summary: a5 invocation shim duplicates argv handling outside the .hexa file → risk of drift if either side changes. acceptable as belt-and-suspenders only because direct invocation also fixed via a1+a2+a3.

dedupe_method: (1) merge a7 into a1+a2+a3 as helper-extraction (Tier-2 cleanup); (2) cluster Track-A 4 family: parser-core (a1/a2/a3), test (a4), wrapper (a5), CI (a6); (3) Tier 분류 by minimal-fix-now vs hardening: parser-core fix is blocker-removal, test/CI is regression armor, shim is fallback.

tier_1_immediate_impl.rationale: runner-agnostic — works on every argv layout without env probe. drops .hexa elements whose basename matches the script's own. SINGLE most-impactful axis: removes the bug for all 3 invocation paths simultaneously.
tier_1_immediate_impl.line_precise_diff_intent: Replace lines 814-819 (`let mut cli = []; let mut ai = 2; while ai < len(raw_args) { cli.push(raw_args[ai]); ai = ai + 1 }`) with: `let mut cli = []; let self_base = "omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa"; let mut ai = 0; while ai < len(raw_args) { let a = raw_args[ai]; let is_self = ends_with(a, self_base); let is_runner = (a == "hexa") || (a == "run"); if !is_self && !is_runner { cli.push(a) } ai = ai + 1 }`. Net: ~12 LoC, replaces magic constant `2` with content-aware filter.

tier_1_immediate_impl.rationale: escape hatch when a2 heuristic misfires (b3 pathological case). user-explicit precedence over auto-detect.
tier_1_immediate_impl.line_precise_diff_intent: Add early-pass over raw_args BEFORE self-path-strip: scan for `--input-mode=raw` (skip filtering entirely) / `--input-mode=local` (ai=0) / `--input-mode=remote` (ai=2). When present, bypass a2 logic and use legacy slice. ~10 LoC, inserts before line 814.

tier_1_immediate_impl.rationale: defense-in-depth: env var HEXA_FORCE_LOCAL=1 + argv[0] basename. only used as TIE-BREAKER when a2 produces ambiguous result (≥2 .hexa elements).
tier_1_immediate_impl.line_precise_diff_intent: Add helper `_detect_runner() -> string` returning {"remote","local","unknown"} from env+argv[0]. invoked only inside a2 when 2+ .hexa elements survive filtering. ~15 LoC, new fn before main().

tier_1_immediate_impl.rationale: concrete falsifier — 3 invocation paths × `--selftest` must exit 0 and emit pass marker. without this, fix is asserted-not-tested.
tier_1_immediate_impl.line_precise_diff_intent: In _selftest(), add stanza after existing tests: simulate raw_args = [["hexa","run","tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa","--selftest"], ["hexa","run","--","tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa","--selftest"], ["tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa","--selftest"]] — each → _normalize_args() must yield ["--selftest"]. assert + emit `__OMEGA_CYCLE_ATLAS_INGEST__ PASS` then exit(0). ~18 LoC inside _selftest().

tier_1_immediate_impl.rationale: belt-and-suspenders fallback for callers who can't depend on .hexa parser fix landing first. shell script, zero hexa dep.
tier_1_immediate_impl.line_precise_diff_intent: NEW FILE tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.sh: `#!/usr/bin/env bash; export HEXA_RUNNER=remote; exec hexa run tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa "$@"`. ~20 LoC including header, error handling, and chmod +x note.

tier_2_backlog.blocker: requires CI infra (GitHub Actions or local-make) wired up for the repo; defer until Tier-1 a4 selftest passes manually 3-path
refactor — extract a1+a2+a3 logic into _normalize_args() once Tier-1 lands; cleanup pass only

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 7 axes (parser-core / test / wrapper / CI) + Track-B 6 limits — second-pass produced no new axis (env probe + self-path strip + flag override = exhaustive within current hexa runtime). all Tier-1 axes ≤ 20 LoC.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT — Tier-1 5개 / Tier-2 2개 / Track-B 6 limits. ordinal=ω (terminal: no transcendental jump, just bounded patch).

concrete_falsifier.name: F_argfix_3path
concrete_falsifier.spec: Under all 3 invocation paths {P1=`hexa run tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa --selftest`, P2=`hexa run -- tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa --selftest`, P3=`HEXA_FORCE_LOCAL=1 ./tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa --selftest`}, the process MUST exit 0 AND emit literal substring `__OMEGA_CYCLE_ATLAS_INGEST__ PASS` to stdout. Any non-zero exit OR missing marker on any path = falsifier triggered.
concrete_falsifier.cleanup_action: rollback parser change, re-open ω-cycle for revised runner-detection heuristic

first_impl_target.name: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa
first_impl_target.scope: Tier-1 a2+a3+a1+a4 in single .hexa file (~55 LoC delta) + a5 new .sh shim (~20 LoC)
first_impl_target.primary_diff_target: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa lines 814-819 (replace `let mut ai = 2` legacy slice with content-aware filter)
first_impl_target.verification: _selftest() 3-path corpus must exit 0 + emit `__OMEGA_CYCLE_ATLAS_INGEST__ PASS` for each. Then re-run blocked parent ω-cycles (atlas_ingest, atlas_ingest_tool_evolution) — atlas.append.<slug>.n6 shards must emit successfully.
